rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes and implementation annexes in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
The authoritative source for component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections is the input AADL model
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that the inserted AGREE annex blocks are placed in the correct scope within the AADL model
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
The authoritative source for component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections is the input AADL model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid component type scopes
Do not place implementation-level bindings in invalid implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
